purpose: Test if A2 catches duplicate bug reports (different wording, same root issue)
tier_explanation: Hard mode — reduced keyword overlap, indirect module names, multi-perspective descriptions
source: Codex-generated alternative phrasings of REAL CPI bugs (cpi_duplicate_bug_reports_hard.json)

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: Product group change leaves dependent classification blank
items.duplicate_description: While adding product master data in Pimcore, I selected the high level Product Master Group first, but the next classification field did not refresh. Because the SAG value is not derived from the PMG, the user can continue with incomplete hierarchy data.

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: Second level product family options are not restricted by selected group
items.duplicate_description: In PIM product creation, after choosing the PMG, the following product family list is not getting calculated from the selected group. This can allow wrong SAG mapping for SKU/NPI records.

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: Hierarchy selection incomplete during product setup
items.duplicate_description: The form is allowing PMG selection, but the dependent SAG list is not populated for the chosen PMG. User cannot complete correct CUMI product hierarchy before moving the item in workflow.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description: Created a New NPI under Coated Industrial > Belt, but mail goes to different Product Developers such as Bonded Industrial and Coated Retail owners.
items.duplicate_title: Coated Belt NPI lands in unrelated developer inbox
items.duplicate_description: I created a test NPI for Coated Industrial belt. The email did not go to the developer mapped for that PMG/SAG and instead reached users from other product lines. The routing looks based on a wrong master mapping.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description: Created a New NPI under Coated Industrial > Belt, but mail goes to different Product Developers such as Bonded Industrial and Coated Retail owners.
items.duplicate_title: New item request is assigned by wrong product category
items.duplicate_description: NPI notification is not following the selected product category. For a Coated Industrial item, Pimcore sent approval mail to Bonded/retail related Product Developers, so the correct team is not notified.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description: Created a New NPI under Coated Industrial > Belt, but mail goes to different Product Developers such as Bonded Industrial and Coated Retail owners.
items.duplicate_title: Notification matrix not matching NPI product hierarchy
items.duplicate_description: During NPI creation, email recipients should be decided from PMG and SAG. Actual mail recipients are from different PMG groups, which may cause another Product Developer to act on the wrong NPI.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer, Product Manager, and Product Head are seeing incorrect or inconsistent workflow actions.
items.duplicate_title: SKU update users can choose actions from another stage
items.duplicate_description: In the SKU maintenance flow, the available action menu does not match the current user's responsibility. The developer screen shows enrichment action, while manager screens show approval style options, which can push SKU to the wrong status.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer, Product Manager, and Product Head are seeing incorrect or inconsistent workflow actions.
items.duplicate_title: Returned SKU does not show the revised enrichment completion action
items.duplicate_description: After Product Head sends an SKU back, Product Manager does not receive the expected completion action for the update cycle. The same generic action labels are shown, so it is unclear how to submit corrected SKU enrichment.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer, Product Manager, and Product Head are seeing incorrect or inconsistent workflow actions.
items.duplicate_title: Role button matrix is swapped in SKU change journey
items.duplicate_description: The SKU workflow menu appears to be reading the wrong stage/role combination. Product Developer, Product Manager, and Product Head do not get their expected action labels as per the CPI workflow sheet.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: Reopen creates unexpected child object in product tree
items.duplicate_description: After using reopen on an existing product, I noticed an additional child SKU under the same product. This looks like system-generated duplicate SKU, and the child screen also contains ERP/DAM sections.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: Product rework flow is exposing ERP and asset tabs at SKU level
items.duplicate_description: When a product is reopened for correction, Pimcore opens a new SKU record and that SKU has ERP and DAM tabs. Expected is only reopening the existing product workflow without adding a new SKU node.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: Extra SKU appears after sending approved product back
items.duplicate_description: For approved product, on reopen action the hierarchy changes. A new SKU gets created and it has tabs normally not expected there, including ERP and DAM. This may confuse ERP and DAM users because the original SKU is no longer the only child.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed, actions disappear, and approving again shows both InReview and Product Data Approved statuses.
items.duplicate_title: Product gets stuck after rework request from manager
items.duplicate_description: On Product Manager login, the rework/reopen action fails first with a mail configuration error and then the product stops showing valid workflow options. After that the same product shows mixed approval state.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed, actions disappear, and approving again shows both InReview and Product Data Approved statuses.
items.duplicate_title: Reopen attempt corrupts product workflow marking
items.duplicate_description: The product workflow is not stable after trying to send the product back. Pimcore says the transition is not enabled, and later the item displays both review and approved status indicators together.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed, actions disappear, and approving again shows both InReview and Product Data Approved statuses.
items.duplicate_title: Missing email template blocks product return flow
items.duplicate_description: Product Manager cannot reopen the workflow because the email template path is empty. Retrying does not recover the workflow; action buttons vanish and the product status becomes inconsistent.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow for Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail, email goes to wrong users, post approval user Product Head is missing in mail body, and notification sent to Product Head Industrial opens with error.
items.duplicate_title: Retail SKU approval notification opens error for Industrial user
items.duplicate_description: A Bonded Retail SKU change produced an in-app notification for Product Head Industrial. When that user clicks the notification, Pimcore shows an error. The same transition should have gone only to the mapped retail users.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow for Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail, email goes to wrong users, post approval user Product Head is missing in mail body, and notification sent to Product Head Industrial opens with error.
items.duplicate_title: Change SKU mail body is missing next approver information
items.duplicate_description: After Product Developer submits Change SKU, the email content does not show the Product Head as the post approval user. Also the recipient list does not match the SKU PMG/retail mapping.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow for Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail, email goes to wrong users, post approval user Product Head is missing in mail body, and notification sent to Product Head Industrial opens with error.
items.duplicate_title: SKU change request routed to DISC manager for retail item
items.duplicate_description: For Super Abrasive Retail SKU, approval email reached DISC Manager instead of Product Manager Retail. This seems to be the wrong recipient lookup for the Change SKU workflow.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains a line asking to send the product to Product Manager for review.
items.duplicate_title: DAM approval mail is still using earlier workflow instruction
items.duplicate_description: The email received at DAM stage contains text asking to send product back to Product Manager. This appears to be an old template line because the current flow should go from DAM Manager to publish.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains a line asking to send the product to Product Manager for review.
items.duplicate_title: Publish stage email suggests wrong next reviewer
items.duplicate_description: After Marketing Manager approval, DAM Manager receives email with Product Manager review instruction. Since DAM Manager is the final publish-to-web approver, that Product Manager sentence should not appear.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains a line asking to send the product to Product Manager for review.
items.duplicate_title: Incorrect post-approval sentence in DAM notification template
items.duplicate_description: In Prod, DAM notification content has a misleading line about sending the product to Product Manager after approval. The product should be published to web after DAM approval, so the mail template is wrong.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates.
items.duplicate_title: PIM ID lookup allows impossible date range
items.duplicate_description: ERP support can filter PIM IDs with dates later than today's date. The screen then returns no data, but this range should not be selectable because PIM records cannot be created in future.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates.
items.duplicate_title: ERP listing calendar has no upper date limit
items.duplicate_description: The calendar control in ERP PIM ID list lets the user pick future Start Date and End Date. Expected max date should be current date to avoid empty or invalid ERP search results.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates.
items.duplicate_title: Future-dated ERP filter produces misleading blank result
items.duplicate_description: When a future range is selected in ERP form, PIM ID grid becomes empty. It should be prevented at field level by disabling future dates in both date filters.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: ERP screen changes field set after item code creation is enabled
items.duplicate_description: The ERP fields match the NPI creation mapping initially. After publish, when New item code creation is ticked, the form shows additional fields that were not in the client mapping sheet.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: Published product has more ERP attributes than NPI draft
items.duplicate_description: For same product, ERP section in draft NPI and post-publish view are not identical. Five extra ERP attributes appear only after publish, which may make ERP users fill non-required data.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: Client attribute sheet not followed after publish
items.duplicate_description: After product is published and new item code flow is started, ERP tab includes extra fields outside the agreed mapping. The issue is not with data entry; the form configuration changes after status change.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: Approved product loses ERP navigation after visiting assets section
items.duplicate_description: On an ERP approved product, if I open the asset/DAM area once, I cannot switch back to the ERP tab. This is happening for multiple logins, so it seems like tab state issue and not role specific.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: ERP form becomes inaccessible after DAM tab click
items.duplicate_description: After ERP approval, Product Manager and other users should still be able to review ERP values. But once DAM tab is clicked, ERP tab is disabled/non-clickable on the same product.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: Tab switching fails between DAM and ERP on approved item
items.duplicate_description: The product detail tabs are not working correctly after ERP approval. Clicking DAM tab prevents returning to ERP tab, even though ERP details should remain accessible according to permissions.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]. Current value is YS592-Ceramic BELTS, which exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG.
items.duplicate_title: Coated item cannot open because master value is treated invalid
items.duplicate_description: After ERP added item code, the product shifted to Coated folder but cannot be opened cleanly. Pimcore rejects the selected BELT value in the SAG description field even though the value is present in the master sheet.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]. Current value is YS592-Ceramic BELTS, which exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG.
items.duplicate_title: Data model SAG value missing from Pimcore option list
items.duplicate_description: The product has YS592-Ceramic BELTS selected from valid BELT PMG data, but after approval the object throws invalid option error. Looks like SAGDescription options in Pimcore are not synced with the latest master data.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]. Current value is YS592-Ceramic BELTS, which exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG.
items.duplicate_title: NPI to Coated movement fails due to select field validation
items.duplicate_description: When the ERP item code step completes, the NPI record moves to Coated section and then fails on SAGDescription select validation. The value should be accepted because it is a valid PMG/SAG combination.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: Asset tab appears before product reaches approved state
items.duplicate_description: For PM, PH and MM roles, the DAM/asset tab is visible while product is still under approval. As per role matrix, DAM should be hidden until product approval is completed.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: Pre-approval product screen exposes DAM section to business roles
items.duplicate_description: During product workflow testing, users like Product Manager, Product Head and Marketing Manager can see DAM before approval. The tab should only appear after approval and then based on permission.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: DAM visibility starts one workflow stage early
items.duplicate_description: The product UI shows DAM tab before product is approved. This is not expected for PM/PH/MM and may let users check digital asset fields before the product is ready for DAM stage.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: PM cannot verify product image from DAM preview
items.duplicate_description: In Product Manager login, the DAM tab opens but the linked product image preview is blank. The asset is expected to show as thumbnail so PM can verify the correct DAM file.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: Digital asset preview missing only for Product Manager
items.duplicate_description: When checking product DAM details with PM role, image thumbnail is not rendered. The issue impacts review because the PM cannot visually confirm the uploaded digital asset.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: DAM image area is empty on Product Manager product view
items.duplicate_description: The DAM section has product asset information, but thumbnail/image is not displayed for Product Manager user. Expected the same asset preview should be visible for verification.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: Developer role gains asset section after web publish
items.duplicate_description: Once DAM Manager publishes the product, Product Developer login starts showing the digital asset tab. Developer should never get access to DAM fields, even for Published to Web products.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: Published product permission allows Product Developer to edit DAM data
items.duplicate_description: In Prod, Product Developer can open DAM tab and enter values after the product is published. This appears to be a permission leakage after publish status.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: DAM access matrix fails after Publish to Web status
items.duplicate_description: The DAM tab is hidden correctly before publish for Product Developer, but becomes visible after Publish to Web. Access should remain invisible/disabled for Product Developer in all product statuses.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Product feed responds without login credentials
items.duplicate_description: I called the CPI product endpoint without passing authorization details and still received product data. API should block the request instead of returning PMG/SAG/SKU information.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Unauthenticated request can read PIM product records
items.duplicate_description: Product API is not enforcing token validation. Even without a valid auth header, the response contains product master data, which is a security gap for PIM and ERP integration.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Authorization check missing on product data API
items.duplicate_description: The external API for product listing returns data when accessed directly without credentials. Expected unauthorized response should be returned and no SKU/product payload should be exposed.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: Re-imported SKU file completes but gives no user feedback
items.duplicate_description: After deleting child SKUs and uploading the same ERP SKU sheet again, the records are created, but the notification popup has no message. User cannot know from UI whether the import was successful.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: Second upload under same parent shows empty alert
items.duplicate_description: SKU ERP import behaves differently on repeat upload. The same two SKUs are inserted under the product after delete, but Pimcore displays a blank popup instead of success or error text.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: SKU import status text missing when records were previously deleted
items.duplicate_description: If SKUs are imported once, removed from the parent product, and imported again, the import process works but the status message is empty. Expected clear import result message for the ERP SKU upload.
